title: Keir Starmer not ‘jumping’ whenever Donald Trump expects him to
description: Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth says UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is not “jumping” whenever US President Donald Trump expects him to.  Ms Elsworth told Sky News host Peta Credlin there has been a lot of “support” for Keir Starmer on this move.  “There is no doubt, because Sir Keir Starmer is not just offering support at any moment, that this will be difficult for their relationship moving forward.”

title: Keir Starmer has ‘declined’ Trump’s call to send ships to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz
description: Sky News host Peta Credlin says UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has “declined US President Donald Trump’s call” to send ships to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Ms Credlin said it comes as many Gulf states are reportedly urging the United States to neutralise Iran for good.  “They want the United States to stay on course.”

title: Albanese government ‘refusing to guarantee’ Australia won’t need ‘petrol rationing’
description: Sky News host Andrew Bolt says the Albanese government made the “petrol crisis” worse.  Mr Bolt said Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles “added to the panic”.  “By refusing to guarantee we won’t soon need petrol rationing.”

title: Albanese government has the ‘record’ for highest spending, tax revenue and debt
description: Sky News host Sharri Markson says the Albanese government has the “record” for the highest spending, tax revenue, and debt.  Ms Markson said it seems like “voters know this”.   “With a new poll today showing that Australians blame Albanese.”

title: Sky News host blasts Chalmers for using war as ‘shield’ for Australia’s failing economy
description: Sky News host Andrew Bolt blasts Treasurer Jim Chalmers for not preparing the Australian economy better.   “The Reserve Bank today warned of possible recession even as it lifted interest rates for the second time in a row,” Mr Bolt said.   “As the Reserve Bank Governor today admitted, this rate rise had more to do with the inflation that was already rising before the war.   “Chalmers today used the war as a shield.   “Inflation will only get worse as this war goes on and petrol and diesel prices stay high.   “This war isn't the reason for this disaster today. The real disaster is that this government didn't prepare for surprises like this, and now we'll pay.”

title: A farmer's plea prompts Minns' fuel supply intervention after federal government fails
description: NSW Farmers Association President Xavier Martin has vowed to intervene in the fuel crisis and order deliveries to shortage areas.   “What we’re dealing with is a premier that quickly recognised the federal government is way out of its depth in terms of responding to what we’ve got here in NSW,” Mr Martin told Sky News host Peta Credlin.   “We’ve got a serious problem.   “We are expecting him to get active this week.”

title: ‘Riddled with fraud’: ‘Shonky’ NDIS providers called out for ‘breaking’ the scheme
description: Activist Drew Pavlou claims the NDIS scheme is “unfortunately riddled with fraud”.   “I think it's riddled with fraud to the very core of the scheme,” Mr Pavlou told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.   “They created this market mechanism where severely disabled people … were expected to go around and shop for providers, and that has just created this massive vulnerability.  “It’s become this money incinerator.   “I think the entire scheme has been broken from the start.”

title: Jim Chalmers considered ‘lucky’ for blaming Iran war on inflation rate spike
description: Sky News Political Editor Andrew Clennell calls Treasurer Jim Chalmers “lucky” for blaming inflation on the Iran war.   ”Jim has kind of got lucky a little bit as the world has got unlucky,” Mr Clennell told Sky News host Peta Credlin.   “He’s lucky in that he’s got something to blame.”

title: Lefties Losing It: Jimmy Kimmel plays the victim… again
description: Sky News host Rita Panahi has criticised Jimmy Kimmel for playing the victim again.

title: ‘Truckies are right on the edge’: Fuel crisis threatens businesses and farmers nationwide
description: Sky News host Paul Murray reacts to a multitude of reports stating fuel shortages are affecting everyday Aussies as well as farmers across the country.   “Dozens of petrol stations are starting to run out here,” Mr Murray said.   “They, of course, put it down to panic buying, but the reason they’re not getting restocked is part of the problem that is playing out across the country right now.  “Farmers need to buy huge amounts of diesel to keep our food secure.  “The ABC are talking about how truckies are right on the edge, this is only two weeks into a war … there are truck drivers that are saying that their business that has been going for 20 years, could disappear.”

title: Australians have ‘lost faith’ in RBA after second rate rise
description: Centre for Independent Studies Director Michael Stutchbury analyses what RBA Governor Michelle Bullock had to say when she announced the second rate rise.   “The reason why we are having this is because people, voters, businesses are sort of losing a bit of faith in the ability or the commitment of the government, and to an extent the Reserve Bank, to really get on top of this inflation,” Mr Stutchbury told Sky News host Sharri Markson.  “If people think that inflation is going to keep on going, then it becomes a bit self-fulfilling.”

title: ‘I don’t know what to do’: Reality of Australia’s fuel crisis exposed as Aussies struggle
description: Warramunda Estate Winery Owners Robert Magdziarz and his daughter Olivia Zak-Magdziarz are facing a dire situation due to the ongoing fuel crisis in Australia.   “Honestly, I’m without words. We have one chance a year, that is it, and that chance starts tomorrow,” Ms Zak-Magdziarz told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “I don’t know what to do, it’s quite scary.”

title: ‘We’re paying the price’: Jim Chalmers in a ‘state of denial’ with the economy
description: Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson slams Treasurer Jim Chalmers for being responsible for the crisis “Australians are living”.    “I just think the treasurer is in a state of denial of the scale of the problem that he has created,” Mr Wilson told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “He has kept pouring debt onto the Australian economy, thinking it is going to solve the economy.”

title: ‘An uphill battle’: Questions rise as to whether Jess Wilson can ‘put up a fight’ to Jacinta Allan
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond sympathises with Victorians because of their premier, Jacinta Allan .  “Victorians, I think, need something to take the edge off,” Mr Bond said.   “They’ve got to live with Jacinta Allan for heaven’s sake.  “The most unpopular premier in the country, according to a poll last week.  “What are the chances of the Coalition … actually being able to put up the fight to Labor and Jacinta Allan?”

title: New evidence suggests medicinal cannabis does not treat mental illnesses
description: Sky News host James Macpherson looks at reports indicating medical cannabis does not treat mental health conditions.  “Is it effective? That’s the question,” Mr Macpherson said.   “A study published today … says no, the review found that there was no evidence to support the idea that medicinal cannabis helped treat anxiety, depression or PTSD.  “If anything, the evidence says it makes those things worse.”
